justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Prototype pollution identified by name in title and throughout. (2) __proto__ key attack vector explicitly explained with iteration via `for (const key in source)`. (3) Global Object.prototype impact explained — 'Every object in the process now has isAdmin === true'. (4) Safe merge fix recommended — guard with Object.hasOwn + reject __proto__/constructor/prototype keys, plus schema validation with zod. (5) Specific attack payload: `{"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}` with curl reproduction. (6) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Textbook-quality explanation of the prototype pollution mechanism — traces the full chain from `for..in` through `target["__proto__"]` resolving to `Object.prototype`.
Concrete curl reproduction command makes the vulnerability immediately verifiable.
Goes beyond the minimum by identifying mass assignment (over-posting) as a second critical finding — a real additional risk.
Identifies the subtle falsy-value bug in `if (!target[key])` which would corrupt boolean `false` and numeric `0` preferences — excellent secondary finding.
Fix recommendation is layered: immediate guard (hasOwn + key blocklist) plus strategic fix (schema validation with zod) — pragmatic and thorough.
Correctly notes that `constructor` and `prototype` keys also need blocking, not just `__proto__`.
